targetArtifacts.description: canonical local-first multi-agent pipeline scope contract

acceptanceCriteria: Scope doc defines bounded local-first collaboration and trusted Rust/local boundary
Non-goals forbid hidden agents, auto-apply, auto-merge, command bridges, release automation, and Godot replacement claims
requiredEvidence: PR evidence links scope doc and focused regression

acceptanceCriteria: Task ids, roles, owners, statuses, dependencies, targets, acceptance criteria, evidence, blockers, and timestamps are represented
Task board remains artifact/state only and does not execute work or spawn agents
requiredEvidence: Generated-state audit showing only fixture-scoped board is tracked

targetArtifacts.description: generated QA queue path that remains ignored unless fixture-scoped later

acceptanceCriteria: QA queue issue receives its own fixture-scoped contract before display or execution
No browser or hidden worker may execute queue items

guardrails: Task board is local artifact/state only, not hidden agent execution
Agent outputs remain untrusted until Rust/local validation and review-gated apply or promotion
Browser/dashboard/Studio surfaces may inspect task state only when separately scoped as read-only/draft-only
boundary: Fixture-scoped production task board artifact only; it does not execute work, spawn agents, apply changes, merge PRs, write trusted browser state, or mutate source files.
